case_name: In Re: Medical Review Panel Proceeding of the Family of Hai Nguyen .

summary.brief: Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Ms. Nguyen visited the emergency room at Tulane Medical Center accompanied by her daughter, Nga Nguyen (“Nga”). She had fallen at her home.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Nguyen returned to Tulane Medical Center on May 15, 2019, complaining of shortness of breath and lower extremity swelling.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Zamir ordered continuing dialysis and noted “non-gangrene” on her lower extremities after a normal inspection.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On June 3, 2019, Ms. Nguyen sought further treatment with Dr. Vu Mai at Westbank Medical and Walk-In Clinic.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Nguyen was “present for a [follow up] for gangrene in the toes and heel.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Nguyen’s leg was amputated below the knee on July 1, 2019.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Nguyen died from a heart attack on March 27, 2020.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The judgment dismissing Appellants’ petition filed more than one year later on June 15, 2021 is affirmed.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Based on the record before us, we find that Ms. Nguyen had the necessary constructive knowledge to begin tolling the one-year prescriptive period on June 3, 2019.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.chief_complaint: Fall at home resulting in bruising and subarachnoid hemorrhage
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented to the emergency room after a fall at home, accompanied by her daughter.

focused_exam.extremities: Bruising noted, gangrene not documented at this visit

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Fall with head injury and potential complications

requestables.name: CT Head
requestables.description: CT scan of the head to assess for hemorrhage

requestables.name: Nephrology Consult
requestables.description: Consultation with nephrology for dialysis management

reveal.decision_impact: Continued dialysis without further vascular investigation

timeline_phases.name: First ER Visit
timeline_phases.description: Initial presentation to the emergency room after a fall

timeline_phases.name: Second ER Visit
timeline_phases.description: Return to ER with new symptoms

key_events.event: Admitted for shortness of breath and swelling

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with diabetes and peripheral vascular disease presenting with lower extremity issues
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in evaluating the patient's lower extremity condition?

options.description: Order vascular imaging to assess for gangrene
options.clinical_reasoning: Necessary to rule out vascular compromise given patient's history

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant continued dialysis without further vascular imaging

expected_action_court.description: Vascular imaging should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Patient's history and symptoms warranted further investigation

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to investigate potential gangrene led to delayed diagnosis
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Vascular imaging would have identified gangrene earlier

patient_outcome.description: Patient's condition worsened, leading to leg amputation and eventual death

key_findings: Claim prescribed due to constructive knowledge on June 3, 2019
No breach of duty found within prescriptive period

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Earlier imaging could have prevented amputation

factual_timeline.event: Patient returned to ER with new symptoms
Patient visited Dr. Mai for worsening symptoms

court_findings.finding: Claim prescribed due to constructive knowledge
